narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout
narratives.thesis: Demand for AI chips and related infrastructure continues to drive significant growth for semiconductor companies, with NVIDIA leading the charge and other players benefiting from the expanding ecosystem.
watchFor: Hyperscaler capex cuts at next earnings
NVDA revenue growth slows below 20%
China export controls expand to HBM

narratives.name: GLP-1 Obesity Wave
narratives.thesis: The demand for GLP-1 obesity and diabetes drugs continues to drive significant growth for pharmaceutical companies, with new approvals and strong endorsements fueling investor confidence.

Technology.thesis: The Technology sector remains broadly bullish, driven by the insatiable demand for AI infrastructure and related software, with strong profit growth in key areas offsetting some concerns about valuation and competition.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing, hurting growth stock multiples
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging — would disrupt AI supply chain

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: The Consumer Cyclical sector is showing mixed signals. While some areas like e-commerce and specialty retail are seeing growth, residential construction faces headwinds from higher rates, and auto manufacturers are navigating a complex transition.
watchFor: Unexpectedly strong retail sales data (>0.5% MoM) — signals consumer resilience
Further interest rate hikes from Fed — would dampen consumer spending and housing
Significant decline in consumer confidence index — indicates future spending slowdown

Communication Services.thesis: The Communication Services sector is mixed. While entertainment content continues to expand and attract audiences, the digital advertising space faces challenges from AI and privacy concerns.
watchFor: Major streaming services report subscriber declines — signals content fatigue
New privacy regulations impacting digital ad revenue — hurts ad-dependent platforms
Economic slowdown impacting discretionary consumer spending on entertainment

Industrials.thesis: The Industrials sector is experiencing mixed trends. Aerospace & Defense benefits from geopolitical spending, while integrated freight faces a complex environment. Farm and heavy construction machinery shows divergence between strong construction and weak agriculture.
watchFor: De-escalation of geopolitical conflicts — would reduce defense spending
Significant downturn in global trade volumes — hurts freight & logistics
Sharp rise in commodity prices — increases input costs for machinery manufacturers

Healthcare.thesis: The Healthcare sector is bullish, primarily driven by the ongoing GLP-1 obesity drug wave and strong performance from healthcare plans, with medical device companies also showing resilience.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors emerge with superior efficacy/safety profiles — would challenge leaders
Increased regulatory scrutiny on drug pricing — could cap pharma profits
Unexpected rise in medical loss ratios for healthcare plans — hurts profitability

Financials.thesis: The Financials sector is bullish, driven by regulatory clarity for major banks, strong performance in credit services, and momentum in asset management, despite some warnings about high yields.
watchFor: Unexpected interest rate cuts by the Fed — hurts bank net interest margins
Significant increase in loan defaults or credit losses — impacts credit services
New, stricter financial regulations — could increase compliance costs

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The Consumer Defensive sector is neutral, with discount stores showing some resilience against inflation, but broader consumer spending concerns remain.
watchFor: Sharp decline in inflation — reduces appeal of discount stores
Significant increase in unemployment — impacts discretionary spending
New government stimulus boosting consumer purchasing power

Utilities.thesis: The Utilities sector is bullish, driven by strong performance in renewable energy and independent power producers, with a focus on sustainable energy solutions.
watchFor: Sharp decline in natural gas prices — reduces incentive for renewables
Regulatory changes unfavorable to renewable energy projects
Significant increase in interest rates — raises cost of capital for utility projects

Basic Materials.thesis: The Basic Materials sector is neutral, with rare earth elements facing geopolitical headwinds despite some positive analyst ratings.
watchFor: De-escalation of trade tensions with China — could ease rare earth supply concerns
New discoveries of rare earth deposits outside of China — diversifies supply
Significant drop in demand for high-tech products using rare earths

Precious Metals.thesis: The Precious Metals sector is mixed. Gold is seeing renewed interest amidst ceasefire hopes, while silver faces uncertainty after a significant price drop.
watchFor: Sharp increase in inflation expectations — boosts gold as inflation hedge
Significant weakening of the US Dollar — makes gold more attractive
Global geopolitical tensions escalate — increases safe-haven demand for gold
